issues.description: Content-Type assertion checks for exact string "application/json" but axum 0.8 may include a charset suffix ("application/json; charset=utf-8") depending on response path, which would cause a brittle test failure.
issues.suggestion: Use a starts_with or contains check instead: `assert!(response.headers().get("content-type").unwrap().to_str().unwrap().starts_with("application/json"));`

issues.description: No test for the unhealthy/degraded path (HTTP 503 with {"status":"degraded"}). The spec's NFR-2 only requires the healthy-path test, but the degraded path has no coverage — a regression in the error branch (e.g. wrong status string or status code) would go undetected.
issues.suggestion: Add a second test using a StateStore mock whose health_check() returns Err, then assert status 503 and body {"status":"degraded", "version": ...}.

issues.description: to_bytes(response.into_body(), usize::MAX) uses an unbounded limit. While safe in a test context, it reads oddly and could mask issues if the health response were ever unexpectedly large.
issues.suggestion: Use a reasonable bound like 1024 or 4096 — the expected JSON body is under 100 bytes.
